danger_signals.visual_form: Track-side barriers, oncoming traffic, environmental hazards
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of speed, damage to bike, stage failure if collision severe

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer display counting down during stage progression
danger_signals.meaning: Must complete stage within time limit or lose opportunity to advance

danger_signals.visual_form: Rival motorcyclists visible ahead on track
danger_signals.meaning: Must overtake and maintain lead position to win race

danger_signals.visual_form: Bike slowing visually on screen, speed gauge dropping
danger_signals.meaning: Rider is losing velocity due to track conditions, collision, or poor handling

safety_signals.visual_form: Open road with minimal obstacles, smooth terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to accelerate and maintain high speed

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual effect when reaching 280 km/h threshold, speed gauge enhancement
safety_signals.meaning: Bike enters enhanced performance state allowing 324 km/h maximum speed

safety_signals.visual_form: Wide track sections, good visibility, minimal traffic
safety_signals.meaning: Easier terrain for stage progression, lower collision risk

faction_indicators.color_identity: Rothmans Honda NSR500 color scheme (cover art reference)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Motorcycle design and livery matching real-world racing aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival motorcycle riders with distinct bike designs and colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Billboards featuring sponsor brands (Cibie, Sega game references in VC version)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied by track region (Africa, Asia, Americas, Europe)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Environmental theming distinct to continental setting
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Track-specific visual design, terrain type, obstacle placement, architectural style

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Simpler track layout, wider roads, fewer obstacles, shorter stage progression (6 stages)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent AI less aggressive, collision consequences less severe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased track complexity, more environmental hazards, narrower passages, stage count increases (10-14 stages)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent positioning more competitive, tighter margins for error

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maximum track complexity, dense obstacles, challenging terrain, longest progression (18 stages)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents race most aggressively, collision risk highest, time pressure most intense

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Speed gauge visual enhancement, screen scrolling speed increases, visual effects intensify
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Track hazards become more dangerous at higher velocity, precision handling critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bike component upgrades reflected in visual appearance and performance indicator changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced handling, acceleration, and top speed based on purchased components

environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct race courses (Africa, Asia, Americas, Europe) each with unique visual identity, terrain type, architecture, and difficulty progression that tells story of global motorcycle racing championship

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Real-world brand names and Sega game references on track-side billboards establish racing world authenticity and game universe connectivity

environmental_storytelling.example: Stage count progression (6→10→14→18 stages) visually communicated through course selection menu, showing player's journey from beginner to elite racer
Cover art featuring Shinichi Itoh's Rothmans Honda NSR500 colorway establishes grounding in authentic motorcycle racing culture and sponsors

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Screen scrolls faster, bike sprite leans forward, speed gauge rises visually
Screen scroll slows, bike sprite becomes more upright, speed gauge drops
Bike sprite leans into turn, handling becomes more sensitive, risk of losing control increases
Maintain close proximity to player, aggressive line positioning, attempt to block player advancement
Fall behind player bike, become less aggressive, reduce screen presence
Visual scaling indicates proximity, collision audio cues, screen shake effect on impact

notes: Super Hang-On uses pseudo-3D scaling technology (Sega Super Scaler) to create illusion of depth. Danger and safety signals are primarily conveyed through track layout design, obstacle placement, and speed mechanics rather than explicit UI warnings. The game communicates world-state through continental theming and progressive difficulty rather than narrative factions. Genesis version includes sponsor mode adding progression narrative through bike customization. Danger is implicit in collision physics and time pressure rather than explicit enemy threat.
